    40	
    41	interface SessionBootstrapArgs {

    44	
    45	interface SessionBootstrapResult {

    66	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    67	
    68	function extractData(response: MCPResponse): Record<string, unknown> {
    69	  try {
    70	    const text = response?.content?.[0]?.text;
    71	    if (typeof text === 'string') {
    72	      const parsed = JSON.parse(text);
    73	      return parsed?.data ?? parsed ?? {};
    74	    }
    75	  } catch { /* parse failed */ }
    76	  return {};
    77	}
    78	
    79	function extractHints(data: Record<string, unknown>): string[] {
    80	  if (Array.isArray(data.hints)) return data.hints as string[];
    81	  return [];
    82	}
    83	
    84	function extractCachedSummary(
    85	  data: Record<string, unknown>,
    86	): CachedSessionSummaryDecision | null {
    87	  const candidate = data.cachedSummary;
    88	  if (candidate && typeof candidate === 'object') {
    89	    return candidate as CachedSessionSummaryDecision;
    90	  }
    91	  return null;
    92	}
    93	
    94	function buildNextActions(
    95	  resumeData: Record<string, unknown>,
    96	  healthData: Record<string, unknown>,
    97	  structuralContext: StructuralBootstrapContract,
    98	): string[] {
    99	  const nextActions = new Set<string>();
   100	
   101	  if (resumeData.error) {
   102	    nextActions.add('Call `session_resume({ specFolder })` directly to inspect the detailed resume failure.');
   103	  }
   104	
   105	  if (healthData.error) {
   106	    nextActions.add('Call `session_health()` directly to inspect the current health-check failure.');
   107	  }
   108	
   109	  if (structuralContext.recommendedAction) {
   110	    nextActions.add(structuralContext.recommendedAction);
   111	  }
   112	
   113	  if (structuralContext.status === 'ready') {
   114	    nextActions.add('Use `session_resume({ specFolder })` when you need the fuller merged recovery payload.');
   115	  } else if (structuralContext.status === 'stale') {
   116	    nextActions.add('Run `code_graph_scan` if you need fresh structural context, then call `session_bootstrap()` again.');
   117	  } else {
   118	    nextActions.add('If structural context matters for this task, run `code_graph_scan` and then re-run `session_bootstrap()`.');
   119	  }
   120	
   121	  const healthStatus = typeof healthData.status === 'string' ? healthData.status : null;
   122	  if (healthStatus === 'warning' || healthStatus === 'stale') {
   123	    nextActions.add('Call `memory_context({ input: "resume previous work", mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` if you need a deeper state refresh.');
   124	  }
   125	
   126	  return [...nextActions].slice(0, 3);
   127	}
   128	
   129	function extractStructuralTrustFromPayload(
   130	  payload: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null,
   131	): StructuralTrust | null {
   132	  if (!payload) {
   133	    return null;
   134	  }
   135	
   136	  const structuralSection = payload.sections.find((section) =>
   137	    section.key === 'structural-context' && section.structuralTrust,
   138	  );
   139	
   140	  return structuralSection?.structuralTrust ?? null;
   141	}
   142	
   143	function buildStructuralRoutingNudge(
   144	  structuralContext: StructuralBootstrapContract,
   145	): SessionBootstrapResult['structuralRoutingNudge'] | null {
   146	  if (structuralContext.status !== 'ready') {
   147	    return null;
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  return {
   151	    advisory: true,
   152	    readiness: 'ready',
   153	    preferredTool: 'code_graph_query',
   154	    message: 'Advisory only: when the next question is about callers, imports, dependencies, or outline, prefer `code_graph_query` before Grep or Glob.',
   155	    preservesAuthority: 'session_bootstrap',
   156	  };
   157	}
   158

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md | sed -n '260,330p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	Same room. Three wings. The tunnel connects them.
   261	
   262	### Why Structure Matters
   263	
   264	Tested on 22,000+ real conversation memories:
   265	
   266	```
   267	Search all closets:          60.9%  R@10
   268	Search within wing:          73.1%  (+12%)
   269	Search wing + hall:          84.8%  (+24%)
   270	Search wing + room:          94.8%  (+34%)
   271	```
   272	
   273	Wings and rooms aren't cosmetic. They're a **34% retrieval improvement**. The palace structure is the product.
   274	
   275	### The Memory Stack
   276	
   277	| Layer | What | Size | When |
   278	|-------|------|------|------|
   279	| **L0** | Identity — who is this AI? | ~50 tokens | Always loaded |
   280	| **L1** | Critical facts — team, projects, preferences | ~120 tokens (AAAK) | Always loaded |
   281	| **L2** | Room recall — recent sessions, current project | On demand | When topic comes up |
   282	| **L3** | Deep search — semantic query across all closets | On demand | When explicitly asked |
   283	
   284	Your AI wakes up with L0 + L1 (~170 tokens) and knows your world. Searches only fire when needed.
   285	
   286	### AAAK Dialect (experimental)
   287	
   288	AAAK is a lossy abbreviation system — entity codes, structural markers, and sentence truncation — designed to pack repeated entities and relationships into fewer tokens at scale. It is **readable by any LLM that reads text** (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Llama, Mistral) without a decoder, so a local model can use it without any cloud dependency.
   289	
   290	**Honest status (April 2026):**
   291	
   292	- **AAAK is lossy, not lossless.** It uses regex-based abbreviation, not reversible compression.
   293	- **It does not save tokens at small scales.** Short text already tokenizes efficiently. AAAK overhead (codes, separators) costs more than it saves on a few sentences.
   294	- **It can save tokens at scale** — in scenarios with many repeated entities (a team mentioned hundreds of times, the same project across thousands of sessions), the entity codes amortize.
   295	- **AAAK currently regresses LongMemEval** vs raw verbatim retrieval (84.2% R@5 vs 96.6%). The 96.6% headline number is from **raw mode**, not AAAK mode.
   296	- **The MemPalace storage default is raw verbatim text in ChromaDB** — that's where the benchmark wins come from. AAAK is a separate compression layer for context loading, not the storage format.
   297	
   298	We're iterating on the dialect spec, adding a real tokenizer for stats, and exploring better break points for when to use it. Track progress in [Issue #43](https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace/issues/43) and [#27](https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace/issues/27).
   299	
   300	### Contradiction Detection (experimental, not yet wired into KG)
   301	
   302	A separate utility (`fact_checker.py`) can check assertions against entity facts. It's not currently called automatically by the knowledge graph operations — this is being fixed (track in [Issue #27](https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace/issues/27)). When enabled it catches things like:
   303	
   304	```
   305	Input:  "Soren finished the auth migration"
   306	Output: 🔴 AUTH-MIGRATION: attribution conflict — Maya was assigned, not Soren
   307	
   308	Input:  "Kai has been here 2 years"
   309	Output: 🟡 KAI: wrong_tenure — records show 3 years (started 2023-04)
   310	
   311	Input:  "The sprint ends Friday"
   312	Output: 🟡 SPRINT: stale_date — current sprint ends Thursday (updated 2 days ago)
   313	```
   314	
   315	Facts checked against the knowledge graph. Ages, dates, and tenures calculated dynamically — not hardcoded.
   316	
   317	---
   318	
   319	## Real-World Examples
   320	
   321	### Solo developer across multiple projects
   322	
   323	```bash
   324	# Mine each project's conversations
   325	mempalace mine ~/chats/orion/  --mode convos --wing orion
   326	mempalace mine ~/chats/nova/   --mode convos --wing nova
   327	mempalace mine ~/chats/helios/ --mode convos --wing helios
   328	
   329	# Six months later: "why did I use Postgres here?"
   330	mempalace search "database decision" --wing orion

   857	            if client_version in SUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSIONS
   858	            else SUPPORTED_PROTOCOL_VERSIONS[0]
   859	        )

   424	def _split_into_segments(text: str) -> List[str]:
   425	    """
   426	    Split text into segments suitable for memory extraction.
   427	
   428	    Tries speaker-turn splitting first (> markers, "Human:", "Assistant:", etc.),
   429	    then falls back to paragraph splitting.

   435	        re.compile(r"^>\s"),  # > quoted user turn
   436	        re.compile(r"^(Human|User|Q)\s*:", re.I),  # Human: / User:
   437	        re.compile(r"^(Assistant|AI|A|Claude|ChatGPT)\s*:", re.I),
   438	    ]

   441	    for line in lines:
   442	        stripped = line.strip()
   443	        for pat in turn_patterns:
   444	            if pat.match(stripped):
   445	                turn_count += 1
   446	                break
   447	
   448	    # If enough turn markers, split by turns
   449	    if turn_count >= 3:
   450	        return _split_by_turns(lines, turn_patterns)

   452	    # Fallback: paragraph splitting
   453	    paragraphs = [p.strip() for p in text.split("\n\n") if p.strip()]
   454	
   455	    # If single giant block, chunk by line groups
   456	    if len(paragraphs) <= 1 and len(lines) > 20:
   457	        segments = []
   458	        for i in range(0, len(lines), 25):
   459	            group = "\n".join(lines[i : i + 25]).strip()
   460	            if group:
   461	                segments.append(group)
   462	        return segments
   463	
   464	    return paragraphs

# Iteration 037: ARCHITECTURE EVOLUTION MAP

## Focus
ARCHITECTURE EVOLUTION MAP: Draw the before/after architecture showing exactly what changes in our memory system with all adoptions applied.

### Before
```text
User / Agent
  -> session_bootstrap + session_resume
  -> memory_context / memory_search / memory_match_triggers
  -> generate-context.js -> markdown memory files -> memory_save indexing
  -> Spec Kit Memory DB
       - semantic + lexical + graph-aware retrieval pipeline
       - cognitive/session state
       - causal edges
       - checkpoints
  -> CocoIndex + code_graph_query/code_graph_context
  -> compact-code-graph plugin injects structural continuity during compaction

Authority model:
  recovery = session_bootstrap/session_resume
  retrieval = memory_context/memory_search/memory_match_triggers
  save = generate-context.js + memory_save
  structure = code graph + CocoIndex
```

### After
```text
User / Agent
  -> same authorities stay in place
  -> session_bootstrap/session_resume gain a tiny memory-protocol hint block
  -> compaction checkpoint producer calls JSON-primary preservation before/at compaction
  -> audit/repair layer grows around saves/checkpoints (WAL-style evidence, repair summaries)
  -> optional wake-up profile becomes a retrieval view over existing memory, not a new store
  -> optional high-stakes verbatim evidence sidecar stores quoted proof snippets only
  -> optional temporal fact sidecar stores invalidatable facts separately from narrative memory

Still not adopted:
  - no wing/hall/room taxonomy as the primary routing surface
  - no AAAK-first recall layer
  - no hook-blocking stop/precompact save flow as the main preservation mechanism
  - no replacement of CocoIndex or code_graph_query with palace navigation
```

## Findings
### Finding 1: Preserve Public’s authority split and add new modules around it, not through it
- **Source**: [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400), [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1129), [memory-triggers.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L184) [SOURCE: session-bootstrap.ts:163, session-resume.ts:400, memory-context.ts:1129, memory-triggers.ts:184]
- **What it does**: Public already separates recovery, retrieval, trigger matching, structured save, checkpoints, and structural code retrieval into distinct authorities.
- **Why it matters**: The winning architecture change is additive: bolt on protocol hints, checkpointing, and sidecars without replacing `session_bootstrap`, `generate-context.js`, CocoIndex, or code graph surfaces.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 2: The biggest before/after delta is a compaction-time JSON-primary checkpoint producer
- **Source**: [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [checkpoints.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/checkpoints.ts#L1412), [mempal_precompact_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh#L71) [SOURCE: spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396, generate-context.js:85, checkpoints.ts:1412, external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh:71]
- **What it does**: MemPalace’s main continuity trick is “save before loss”; Public already has a compaction hook surface, an explicit structured save path, and compressed scoped checkpoints.
- **Why it matters**: After adoption, the architecture changes from passive recovery-only to “recover + preserve before compaction,” but using Public’s deterministic JSON-primary path instead of a blocking model-dependent hook.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 3: Add protocol-teaching and audit/repair as first-class support layers
- **Source**: [external/mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L155), [external/mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L169), [external/mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L70), [external/cli.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/cli.py#L168), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L783) [SOURCE: external/mempalace/mcp_server.py:155, external/mempalace/mcp_server.py:169, external/mempalace/mcp_server.py:70, external/mempalace/cli.py:168, context-server.ts:783]
- **What it does**: MemPalace teaches “how to use memory” in status and wraps writes with WAL-style auditability plus repair tooling; Public already emits hints and has checkpoint/restore machinery, but not as a compact memory-usage protocol surface.
- **Why it matters**: The after-state should include a tiny protocol block in bootstrap/status plus clearer save/checkpoint audit and repair summaries, improving trust without importing palace semantics.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 4: Wake-up should become a retrieval profile, and verbatim should become a bounded evidence sidecar
- **Source**: [external/layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L8), [external/layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L369), [external/searcher.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/searcher.py#L21), [external/general_extractor.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/general_extractor.py#L363), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L415) [SOURCE: external/mempalace/layers.py:8, external/mempalace/layers.py:369, external/mempalace/searcher.py:21, external/mempalace/general_extractor.py:363, session-resume.ts:415]
- **What it does**: MemPalace’s L0-L3 stack is mostly a read model over identity plus ranked snippets, while its ingest still chunks and filters; Public can reproduce the ergonomic gain by adding a wake-up retrieval profile and optional quote-carrying evidence snippets.
- **Why it matters**: The after-state should not become raw-transcript-by-default. It should add a lightweight “startup digest + evidence snippets” layer on top of existing stored memories.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 5: Temporal facts belong in a separate invalidatable sidecar, while taxonomy, AAAK-first recall, and blocking hooks stay out
- **Source**: [external/knowledge_graph.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L56), [external/knowledge_graph.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L121), [external/knowledge_graph.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L180), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md#L292), [external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh#L143), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md#L262) [SOURCE: external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py:56, external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py:121, external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py:180, external/README.md:292, external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh:143, external/README.md:262]
- **What it does**: MemPalace’s cleanest net-new primitive is the temporal fact graph; its weakest transfer candidates are the palace taxonomy as a routing authority, AAAK as a primary recall layer, and stop/precompact blocking as the default save control flow.
- **Why it matters**: The after-state should add one new capability only where it is genuinely new, while explicitly refusing the hype-prone or governance-heavy pieces.
- **Recommendation**: temporal fact sidecar = NEW FEATURE; taxonomy/AAAK-first/blocking hooks = reject
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

## Sources Consulted
- [external/AGENTS.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/AGENTS.md#L1)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.14
- Questions addressed: before-state topology, after-state topology, authority boundaries, preservation delta, sidecar boundaries
- Questions answered: Q1 the storage authority does not change; Q2 the primary adoption is compaction-time JSON-primary checkpointing; Q3 bootstrap/status should teach memory usage more directly; Q4 wake-up and verbatim evidence belong as bounded read models; Q5 temporal facts fit only as a separate invalidatable sidecar
- Novelty justification: This pass does not add new primitives beyond earlier iterations, but it makes the migration concrete by converting the recommendation set into an explicit subsystem map with exact before/after interfaces.
- Operational note: the exact validator command returned `RESULT: PASSED`, but also emitted `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh: line 406: cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`; the phase still infers as Level 1 and only contains [phase-research-prompt.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/phase-research-prompt.md#L1), [research/research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md#L1), and placeholders, so I could not persist this iteration, create the missing Level 3 docs, create `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js`.
- Memory note: I used prior memory only to confirm the existing `005-mempalace` packet handling preference; the architecture findings above are from current-turn source reads.

## Ruled Out
- Replacing `session_bootstrap` or `session_resume` with a palace-style wake-up authority, because Public already has the stronger recovery surface.
- Making wings/halls/rooms the main retrieval router, because Public already has better semantic and structural routing via `memory_search`, CocoIndex, and code graph.
- Adopting AAAK as a primary recall or startup format, because the repo’s own April 7, 2026 correction marks it as lossy and worse than raw mode.
- Using stop/precompact hard blocking as the default preservation contract, because it interrupts the runtime while still depending on model behavior for save quality.
- Treating raw-verbatim-by-default as the new Public storage posture, because the better fit is bounded evidence retention attached to structured memories.

## Reflection
- What worked: reading the Public authority surfaces first and then mapping each MemPalace primitive onto them made the architecture delta precise instead of metaphor-driven.
- What did not work: the sandbox is read-only, so this could not be written back into [research/research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md#L1), and the phase folder is still not in the Level 3 shape requested by the brief.
- What I would do differently: the next write-enabled pass should convert this map into a contract diff section inside the canonical report and then open concrete implementation phases for checkpointing, protocol hints, audit/repair, wake-up profile, and temporal facts.

## Recommended Next Focus

1. define the compaction checkpoint producer contract and kill switch,

# Iteration 037: ARCHITECTURE EVOLUTION MAP

## Focus
ARCHITECTURE EVOLUTION MAP: Draw the before/after architecture showing exactly what changes in our memory system with all adoptions applied.

### Before
```text
User / Agent
  -> session_bootstrap + session_resume
  -> memory_context / memory_search / memory_match_triggers
  -> generate-context.js -> markdown memory files -> memory_save indexing
  -> Spec Kit Memory DB
       - semantic + lexical + graph-aware retrieval pipeline
       - cognitive/session state
       - causal edges
       - checkpoints
  -> CocoIndex + code_graph_query/code_graph_context
  -> compact-code-graph plugin injects structural continuity during compaction

Authority model:
  recovery = session_bootstrap/session_resume
  retrieval = memory_context/memory_search/memory_match_triggers
  save = generate-context.js + memory_save
  structure = code graph + CocoIndex
```

### After
```text
User / Agent
  -> same authorities stay in place
  -> session_bootstrap/session_resume gain a tiny memory-protocol hint block
  -> compaction checkpoint producer calls JSON-primary preservation before/at compaction
  -> audit/repair layer grows around saves/checkpoints (WAL-style evidence, repair summaries)
  -> optional wake-up profile becomes a retrieval view over existing memory, not a new store
  -> optional high-stakes verbatim evidence sidecar stores quoted proof snippets only
  -> optional temporal fact sidecar stores invalidatable facts separately from narrative memory

Still not adopted:
  - no wing/hall/room taxonomy as the primary routing surface
  - no AAAK-first recall layer
  - no hook-blocking stop/precompact save flow as the main preservation mechanism
  - no replacement of CocoIndex or code_graph_query with palace navigation
```

## Findings
### Finding 1: Preserve Public’s authority split and add new modules around it, not through it
- **Source**: [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L400), [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1129), [memory-triggers.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L184) [SOURCE: session-bootstrap.ts:163, session-resume.ts:400, memory-context.ts:1129, memory-triggers.ts:184]
- **What it does**: Public already separates recovery, retrieval, trigger matching, structured save, checkpoints, and structural code retrieval into distinct authorities.
- **Why it matters**: The winning architecture change is additive: bolt on protocol hints, checkpointing, and sidecars without replacing `session_bootstrap`, `generate-context.js`, CocoIndex, or code graph surfaces.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 2: The biggest before/after delta is a compaction-time JSON-primary checkpoint producer
- **Source**: [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [checkpoints.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/checkpoints.ts#L1412), [mempal_precompact_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh#L71) [SOURCE: spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396, generate-context.js:85, checkpoints.ts:1412, external/hooks/mempal_precompact_hook.sh:71]
- **What it does**: MemPalace’s main continuity trick is “save before loss”; Public already has a compaction hook surface, an explicit structured save path, and compressed scoped checkpoints.
- **Why it matters**: After adoption, the architecture changes from passive recovery-only to “recover + preserve before compaction,” but using Public’s deterministic JSON-primary path instead of a blocking model-dependent hook.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 3: Add protocol-teaching and audit/repair as first-class support layers
- **Source**: [external/mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L155), [external/mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L169), [external/mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py#L70), [external/cli.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/cli.py#L168), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L783) [SOURCE: external/mempalace/mcp_server.py:155, external/mempalace/mcp_server.py:169, external/mempalace/mcp_server.py:70, external/mempalace/cli.py:168, context-server.ts:783]
- **What it does**: MemPalace teaches “how to use memory” in status and wraps writes with WAL-style auditability plus repair tooling; Public already emits hints and has checkpoint/restore machinery, but not as a compact memory-usage protocol surface.
- **Why it matters**: The after-state should include a tiny protocol block in bootstrap/status plus clearer save/checkpoint audit and repair summaries, improving trust without importing palace semantics.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 4: Wake-up should become a retrieval profile, and verbatim should become a bounded evidence sidecar
- **Source**: [external/layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L8), [external/layers.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/layers.py#L369), [external/searcher.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/searcher.py#L21), [external/general_extractor.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/general_extractor.py#L363), [session-resume.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts#L415) [SOURCE: external/mempalace/layers.py:8, external/mempalace/layers.py:369, external/mempalace/searcher.py:21, external/mempalace/general_extractor.py:363, session-resume.ts:415]
- **What it does**: MemPalace’s L0-L3 stack is mostly a read model over identity plus ranked snippets, while its ingest still chunks and filters; Public can reproduce the ergonomic gain by adding a wake-up retrieval profile and optional quote-carrying evidence snippets.
- **Why it matters**: The after-state should not become raw-transcript-by-default. It should add a lightweight “startup digest + evidence snippets” layer on top of existing stored memories.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 5: Temporal facts belong in a separate invalidatable sidecar, while taxonomy, AAAK-first recall, and blocking hooks stay out
- **Source**: [external/knowledge_graph.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L56), [external/knowledge_graph.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L121), [external/knowledge_graph.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py#L180), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md#L292), [external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh#L143), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/README.md#L262) [SOURCE: external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py:56, external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py:121, external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py:180, external/README.md:292, external/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh:143, external/README.md:262]
- **What it does**: MemPalace’s cleanest net-new primitive is the temporal fact graph; its weakest transfer candidates are the palace taxonomy as a routing authority, AAAK as a primary recall layer, and stop/precompact blocking as the default save control flow.
- **Why it matters**: The after-state should add one new capability only where it is genuinely new, while explicitly refusing the hype-prone or governance-heavy pieces.
- **Recommendation**: temporal fact sidecar = NEW FEATURE; taxonomy/AAAK-first/blocking hooks = reject
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.14
- Questions addressed: before-state topology, after-state topology, authority boundaries, preservation delta, sidecar boundaries
- Questions answered: Q1 the storage authority does not change; Q2 the primary adoption is compaction-time JSON-primary checkpointing; Q3 bootstrap/status should teach memory usage more directly; Q4 wake-up and verbatim evidence belong as bounded read models; Q5 temporal facts fit only as a separate invalidatable sidecar
- Novelty justification: This pass does not add new primitives beyond earlier iterations, but it makes the migration concrete by converting the recommendation set into an explicit subsystem map with exact before/after interfaces.
- Operational note: the exact validator command returned `RESULT: PASSED`, but also emitted `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh: line 406: cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`; the phase still infers as Level 1 and only contains [phase-research-prompt.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/phase-research-prompt.md#L1), [research/research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md#L1), and placeholders, so I could not persist this iteration, create the missing Level 3 docs, create `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js`.
- Memory note: I used prior memory only to confirm the existing `005-mempalace` packet handling preference; the architecture findings above are from current-turn source reads.

## Ruled Out
- Replacing `session_bootstrap` or `session_resume` with a palace-style wake-up authority, because Public already has the stronger recovery surface.
- Making wings/halls/rooms the main retrieval router, because Public already has better semantic and structural routing via `memory_search`, CocoIndex, and code graph.
- Adopting AAAK as a primary recall or startup format, because the repo’s own April 7, 2026 correction marks it as lossy and worse than raw mode.
- Using stop/precompact hard blocking as the default preservation contract, because it interrupts the runtime while still depending on model behavior for save quality.
- Treating raw-verbatim-by-default as the new Public storage posture, because the better fit is bounded evidence retention attached to structured memories.

## Reflection
- What worked: reading the Public authority surfaces first and then mapping each MemPalace primitive onto them made the architecture delta precise instead of metaphor-driven.
- What did not work: the sandbox is read-only, so this could not be written back into [research/research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md#L1), and the phase folder is still not in the Level 3 shape requested by the brief.
- What I would do differently: the next write-enabled pass should convert this map into a contract diff section inside the canonical report and then open concrete implementation phases for checkpointing, protocol hints, audit/repair, wake-up profile, and temporal facts.

## Recommended Next Focus
Translate this architecture map into an interface-by-interface migration plan:
1. define the compaction checkpoint producer contract and kill switch,
2. define the bootstrap/status protocol-hint payload shape,
3. define the audit/repair evidence model,
4. define the optional wake-up/evidence read models,
5. define the temporal fact sidecar schema and authority boundaries.
